revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==

*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==

*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==
* [[Hwangap]] * {{In title}} [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[sn:ood Rat]] [[en:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq 7]] [[en:Yang Wood Rat]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] *
*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==

*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==
* [[Hwangap]] * {{In title}} [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: 5]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[sn:ood Rat]] [[en:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq 7]] [[en:Yang Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] *
*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
=== Overview === It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==
* [[Hwangap]] * {{In title}} [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:c]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: 5]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[sn:ood Rat]] [[en:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:11 7]] [[en:Yang Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] *
*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==
* [[Hwangap]] * {{In title}} [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: 5]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[sn:ood Rat]] [[en:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:11 7]] [[en:Yang Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] *
*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].[[ja:甲子]]
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==
* [[Hwangap]] * {{In title}} [[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]][[sn:Yang Wood Rat]][[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: 5]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[sn:ood Rat]] [[en:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yang Wood Rat]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[en:Yang Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] <!--Yang Wood Rat-->[[Category:Wood Rat]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] *
*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as <!--please transliterate all the hiragana readings into romaji and provide the kanji--> Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==
* [[Hwangap]] * {{In title}} <!--Yang Wood Rat--> <!--Yang Wood Rat--> <!--Yang Wood Rat--> *
*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=draft|12=draft}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=draft|12=draft}} *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}}

revision.text:
{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ|qq=}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==
* [[Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}
== References == {{Referencesヘルプ|qq=}} <references />
[[zh-min-nan:Kap-chú]] [[zh-yue:甲子]]

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ|qq=}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[sexagenary cycle]] of the [[Chinese zodiac]].
== Western calendar years == A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
== See also ==

*[[Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

== Overview == It was four years after the year of the [[Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[Revolution]] of the [[Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[Taishō|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
