{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}

{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|qq=|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|qq=|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|qq=|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|qq=|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==

==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{Sexagenary cycle comprehensive|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Houtrot (astrologie)]][[cdo:Gák-cṳ̄]][[de:Holz-Ratte]][[en:Wood Rat]][[fr:Rat de bois]][[hak:Kap-chṳ́]][[id:Jiazi]][[it:Jiazi]][[ja:甲子]][[ko:갑자]][[nds:Holt-Rött]][[vi:Giáp Tý]][[za:Gyapceij]][[zh-min-nan:Kap-chú]][[zh-yue:甲子]][[zh:甲子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1190890]] Wikidata name: Yang Wood Rat [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ|qq=}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Houtrot (astrologie)]][[cdo:Gák-cṳ̄]][[de:Holz-Ratte]][[en:Wood Rat]][[fr:Rat de bois]][[hak:Kap-chṳ́]][[id:Jiazi]][[it:Jiazi]][[ja:甲子]][[ko:갑자]][[nds:Holt-Rött]][[vi:Giáp Tý]][[za:Gyapceij]][[zh-min-nan:Kap-chú]][[zh-yue:甲子]][[zh:甲子]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1190890]] Wikidata name: Yang Wood Rat [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ|qq=}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]] of the [[:en:Chinese zodiac|Chinese zodiac]].
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the [[:en:Revolution|Revolution]] of the [[:en:Dynasty|Dynasty]] changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the [[:en:Tian|Tian]] will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Tenchi]], who ascended to the throne after the [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Tenmu]] lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother [[:en:Emperor Kōbun|Otomo]], a prince of the Tenchi line. [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]], and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The [[:en:Yellow Turban Rebellion|Yellow Turban Rebellion]], which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 [[:en:Huangfu Song|Huang Fu Bulk]] biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu]] (now Fukushima Prefecture) in [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in [[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 13 (1924) in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now [[:en:Koshien Stadium|Hanshin Koshien Stadium]]) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*[[:en:Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere]] *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taisho}} 13 (1924) in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}} was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now {{ill|Koshien Stadium|en|Koshien Stadium|WD=Q959811|lt=Hanshin Koshien Stadium}}) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}} of the {{ill|Chinese zodiac|en|Chinese zodiac|WD=Q1976204|lt=Chinese zodiac}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the {{ill|Revolution|en|Revolution|WD=Q10931|lt=Revolution}} of the {{ill|Dynasty|en|Dynasty|WD=Q164950|lt=Dynasty}} changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the {{ill|Tian|en|Tian|WD=Q1364799|lt=Tian}} will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Tenchi}}, who ascended to the throne after the {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Tenmu}} lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Otomo}}, a prince of the Tenchi line. {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin}}, and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The {{ill|Yellow Turban Rebellion|en|Yellow Turban Rebellion|WD=Q751743|lt=Yellow Turban Rebellion}}, which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 {{ill|Huangfu Song|en|Huangfu Song|WD=Q1120512|lt=Huang Fu Bulk}} biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu}} (now Fukushima Prefecture) in {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taisho}} 13 (1924) in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}} was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now {{ill|Koshien Stadium|en|Koshien Stadium|WD=Q959811|lt=Hanshin Koshien Stadium}}) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ|qq=}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}} of the {{ill|Chinese zodiac|en|Chinese zodiac|WD=Q1976204|lt=Chinese zodiac}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the {{ill|Revolution|en|Revolution|WD=Q10931|lt=Revolution}} of the {{ill|Dynasty|en|Dynasty|WD=Q164950|lt=Dynasty}} changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the {{ill|Tian|en|Tian|WD=Q1364799|lt=Tian}} will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Tenchi}}, who ascended to the throne after the {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Tenmu}} lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Otomo}}, a prince of the Tenchi line. {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin}}, and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The {{ill|Yellow Turban Rebellion|en|Yellow Turban Rebellion|WD=Q751743|lt=Yellow Turban Rebellion}}, which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 {{ill|Huangfu Song|en|Huangfu Song|WD=Q1120512|lt=Huang Fu Bulk}} biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu}} (now Fukushima Prefecture) in {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taisho}} 13 (1924) in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}} was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now {{ill|Koshien Stadium|en|Koshien Stadium|WD=Q959811|lt=Hanshin Koshien Stadium}}) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
*{{ill|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|en|Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere|WD=Q1068081|lt=Sixtieth birthday in the Sinosphere}} *{{ill|Kashimura|ja|甲子村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashimura|1=Kasshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577723}} *{{ill|Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|ja|キノエネ醤油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoene Shoyu Co.,Ltd.|1=Kinoene Soy Sauce|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17211706}} *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Wood Rat|kanji=甲子|romaji=kinoe nezumi|onyomi=kōshi|p=jiǎzǐ|w=chia-tzŭ|mi=ʈ͡ʂja̠²¹ tsz̩²¹|y=gaap6 zi2|j=gaap6 zi2|ci=kaːp̚ tsiː²|mc=kæp t͡siʰ|oc-bs=kaːp tsəʔ|qq=}} {{more footnotes needed|date=July 2022|qq=}}
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Wood Rat also known as Wood Rat''' (甲子) is the first combination of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}} of the {{ill|Chinese zodiac|en|Chinese zodiac|WD=Q1976204|lt=Chinese zodiac}}.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Kinoe-ne ***Onyomi 1: Koushi ***Onyomi 2: Kasshi ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): gapja (갑자) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
<!-- == * Yang Wood Rat (甲子) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): jiǎzǐ ** Cantonese (Jyutping): gaap3 zi2 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: きのえね (kinoe-ne) ***Onyomi: こうぼくのね (kōboku-no-ne) ***Onyomi: こうし (kōshi) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): gapja ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Giáp Tý
-->
==Years of Yang Wood Rat== A year of the Wood Rat occurs every 60 years, in a year that ends in 4, according to the following table: {| class="wikitable" |+Associated years !1st millennium !2nd millennium !3rd millennium !4th millennium |- style="vertical-align:top;" | * 4 * 64 * 124 * 184 * 244 * 304 * 364 * 424 * 484 * 544 * 604 * 664 * 724 * 784 * 844 * 904 * 964 | * 1024 * 1084 * 1144 * 1204 * 1264 * 1324 * 1384 * 1444 * 1504 * 1564 * 1624 * 1684 * 1744 * 1804 * 1864 * 1924 * 1984 | * 2044 * 2104 * 2164 * 2224 * 2284 * 2344 * 2404 * 2464 * 2524 * 2584 * 2644 * 2704 * 2764 * 2824 * 2884 * 2944 | * 3004 * 3064 * 3124 * 3184 * 3244 * 3304 * 3364 |}
==Months of Yang Wood Rat== It was four years after the year of the [[:en:Metal Rooster|Metal Rooster]], the year of the {{ill|Revolution|en|Revolution|WD=Q10931|lt=Revolution}} of the {{ill|Dynasty|en|Dynasty|WD=Q164950|lt=Dynasty}} changeover, and was considered the year of the ''revolutionary decree'', or the year of many upheavals, when the {{ill|Tian|en|Tian|WD=Q1364799|lt=Tian}} will is renewed and heavenly commands are given to those who possess virtue ('''Kōji kaihatsu''''). In order to prevent this, the new year has often been held in this year since the Heian period in Japan. Since the Koshi Reform of 1024, the only year prior to the Meiji era (when the reign of the first emperor was abolished with the decree of Issei Ichigen) in which there was no Koshi Reform was in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku}} 7 (1564).<ref>天野忠幸によれば、将軍足利義輝と三好長慶が改元の主導権を巡って争って軍事衝突に発展することを警戒した正親町天皇が甲子改元を見送ったとする（天野忠幸『三好一族と織田信長』（戎光祥出版、2016年） ISBN 978-4-86403-185-1 P47-48）。</ref>
Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]] was also the son of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Tenchi}}, who ascended to the throne after the {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Tenmu}} lineage was severed by the murder of his half-brother {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|WD=Q357242|lt=Otomo}}, a prince of the Tenchi line. {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|en|Emperor Kōnin|WD=Q958662|lt=Emperor Kōnin}}, and it is said that he moved the capital to [[Nagaoka-kyō|Nagaoka-kyo]] in 784, the year of the Revolution, because he was strongly aware of the change of royal line.
The {{ill|Yellow Turban Rebellion|en|Yellow Turban Rebellion|WD=Q751743|lt=Yellow Turban Rebellion}}, which occurred in 184, the last days of the Later Han Dynasty in China and determined the dissolution of the Han Dynasty, was described as "the {{Lang|zh-hant|蒼天已死 黄天當立 歳在甲子 天下大吉|qq=}}" ("Book of the Later Han|The Later Han Dynasty"). Volume 71: Huangfu Song, Zhu Shiyi, Biography 61 {{ill|Huangfu Song|en|Huangfu Song|WD=Q1120512|lt=Huang Fu Bulk}} biography<ref>{{Cite wikisource|title=後漢書/卷71|author=范曄|wslanguage=zh|qq=}}</ref>)". The slogan was.
A hot spring discovered in {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu}} (now Fukushima Prefecture) in {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} in 1384 was named Koshi Onsen (甲子温泉) in honor of the year Koshi.
Ito Daizo (大藏) changed his name to Ito Koshi-taro (Kashitaro/Kinetaro) in honor of the first year of [[:en:Genji (era)|Genji]] (1864), the year of the Koshi.
The baseball stadium built in {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taisho}} 13 (1924) in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}} was named "Koshien Grand Sports Ground" (now {{ill|Koshien Stadium|en|Koshien Stadium|WD=Q959811|lt=Hanshin Koshien Stadium}}) after the Chinese zodiac of that year. <ref>{{Cite web |date=2011-07-06 |title=旧・甲子園ホテル : とっておき旅 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/osaka/feature/kansai1308203483172_02/news/20110706-OYT8T00734.htm |access-date=2013-12-31 |website=[[読売新聞|qq=}}</ref> It is also the name of a place in the city. This year also marks the origin of the name of the Tokyo Kosha Company.
==Days of Yang Wood Rat==
* [[:en:Hwangap|Hwangap]] * {{In title|qq=}} *
